Wrap `return a / b` in a `try` block and add `except ZeroDivisionError:` below it.
Inside the `except` block, `return "undefined"` — the string, not a number.
For `safe_divide_message`, call `safe_divide(a, b)` first and store the result. Then check whether it equals `"undefined"` to pick the right format string.
The success message format is `f"{a} / {b} = {result}"` and the failure format is `f"{a} / {b} is undefined"`.
